question.text: The ICAI Guidance Notes on Accounting for Derivatives recognize which types of hedge accounting?

answer.text: Fair value hedge, Cash flow hedge, and Net investment hedge
Fair value hedge and Cash flow hedge only

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Three types: (1) Fair value hedge — for existing assets/liabilities, (2) Cash flow hedge — for future expected cash flows, (3) Net investment hedge — for foreign operation investments. **The Trap:** All three are recognized — not just one or two. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is from the workbook sample questions. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Hedge accounting allows matching of gains/losses on the hedge instrument with the hedged item, reducing P&L volatility.
